input.prompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
Two separate issues here:

## 1. `StaticResource` doesn't update at runtime

`StaticResource` resolves **once** at XAML load time. Switch to **`DynamicResource`** so bindings re-evaluate when you swap the merged dictionary:

```xml
<!-- ✗ won't update -->
<Label TextColor="{StaticResource PrimaryTextColor}" />

<!-- ✓ updates on theme change -->
<Label TextColor="{DynamicResource PrimaryTextColor}" />
```

Then swap themes in code like:

```csharp
var mergedDicts = Application.Current.Resources.MergedDictionaries;
mergedDicts.Clear();
mergedDicts.Add(new DarkTheme());   // or LightTheme
```

## 2. Android restarts the Activity on UI-mode change

By default, Android **destroys and recreates** the `Activity` when the system dark-mode toggles (it's a `uiMode` configuration change). To prevent this, add `ConfigChanges` to your `MainActivity`:

```csharp
[Activity(
    Theme = "@style/Maui.SplashTheme",
    MainLauncher = true,
    ConfigurationChanges = ConfigChanges.ScreenSize
                         | ConfigChanges.Orientation
                         | ConfigChanges.UiMode        // ← this prevents restart
                         | ConfigChanges.ScreenLayout
                         | ConfigChanges.SmallestScreenSize
                         | ConfigChanges.Density)]
public class MainActivity : MauiAppCompatActivity { }
```

With `ConfigChanges.UiMode` declared, Android won't recreate the activity. You can then detect the change and apply your theme:

```csharp
public override void OnConfigurationChanged(Configuration newConfig)
{
    base.OnConfigurationChanged(newConfig);

    bool isDark = (newConfig.UiMode & UiMode.NightMask) == UiMode.NightYes;
    // swap your ResourceDictionary here
}
```

**TL;DR** — `DynamicResource` for runtime updates, `ConfigChanges.UiMode` to stop the restart.
data.reasoningText:
The user has two issues with their .NET MAUI Android app related to dark mode:

1. **StaticResource vs DynamicResource**: They're using `StaticResource` to bind colors, but `StaticResource` is resolved once at load time and doesn't update when the resource dictionary changes. They need to use `DynamicResource` instead for runtime theme switching.

2. **App restarting on Android dark mode toggle**: When the user toggles dark mode in Android system settings, the app restarts. This is because Android destroys and recreates the activity on configuration changes like `uiMode`. They need to handle this in the Android configuration.


I'll walk through the fixes for both issues.
